definition: The lagged deceleration of OER from ~5.0% to ~3.5-4.0% will deliver 40-80bp of mechanical headline CPI relief, creating a false 'mission accomplished' narrative window likely in Q2-Q4 2026. The 4/6 base rate for premature declarations during Phase 2 goods disinflation is the highest-conviction shared finding across all agents.
evidence: OER deceleration arithmetic: ~5.0% to ~3.5-4.0% delivers 40-80bp mechanical headline CPI relief
4/6 base rate for premature 'mission accomplished' during Phase 2 goods disinflation
1983 and 1995 precedents specifically involved shelter-led headline declines
Fed's revealed reaction function makes premature dovish signaling likely during this window
All four debate reports confirmed as highest-conviction shared finding
open_questions: Will services ex-shelter decelerate enough independently to prevent the trap?
Could productivity growth at 2.5%+ (as in the 1995 exception) validate the easing?
How will the Fed's 2025-2026 framework review interact with the masking window?
